text:
Admission Date:  2015-03-20              Discharge Date:   1997-04-16

Date of Birth:  1980-02-01             Sex:   F

Service: NEUROSURGERY

Allergies:
Vicodin / Penicillins

Attending:Jason
Chief Complaint:
cranial defect

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
10-05 Right cranioplasty


History of Present Illness:
Ms. Williams was a 50 y.o. female who on 1974-08-06 underwent redo
sternotomy/Mitral valve replacement with a [**Street Address(2) 44058**]. [**Male First Name (un) 923**]
mechanical valve with Dr.Beasley. Post-operatively, she was
found to have a Right MCA infarct with associated Left
hemiparesis and seizures.  On 02-01 she underwent a right
hemicraniectomy for cerebral edema and impending cerebral
herniation.  She underwent a tracheostomy and open G-J tube
placement on 12-04.  She was discharged to Mercy Hospital Of Defiance and returned
to Marion General Hospital for further management of a right cranioplasty.

Past Medical History:
Congenital Heart Disease
NSTEMI in 2011-06-16
Pulmonary Hypertension
Hypertension
Dyslipidemia
Paroxysmal Atrial Fibrillation, s/p Ablation
History of Seizures
History of Migraine HA
Morbid Obesity
Hypothyroid
Depression/Anxiety
Osteoarthritis
Chronic Back Pain
Diverticulosis
Sleep Apnea - does not wear CPAP
History of recurrent UTI - most recent in 2008-04-27
Chronic Anemia, unknown etiology
Past Surgical History:
- Atrial Septal Defect Closure and Mitral Valve Cleft repair via
sternotomy in 1972
- AF Ablation at Mercy Hospital Fairfield in 2012
- Left Foot Surgery x 3, most recent 1982
- Left rotator cuff surgery 2015
- Lap Cholecystectomy
- Right Knee Surgery 1993


Social History:
Lives: Home with father
Occupation: On Disability
Cigarettes: Never
ETOH: Denies
Illicit drug use: Denies


Family History:
Father had CABG at age 64. Paternal grandfather
died of MI at age 61.


Physical Exam:
On exam, she is alert.  She has some depression of the
right area of her skull.  Her eyes are to the right, but they do
go fully to the left.  She is able to pick up visual stimulation
on the left, but misses bilateral stimuli.  She is able to point
to stimuli both on her right and left side.  She has some
bifacial weakness with a brisk facial jerk, and has full eye
movement.  She sometimes makes a sound. The movement of her lips
is decreased more on the left, but also a little bit on the
right.  Her right arm and leg function seemed to be well
preserved, but the reflexes are brisk and the toe is up.  On the
left side, she has some movement of the left leg, but very
little
of the left arm.

On Discharge:
A&ox3, mouths words
trach masked
L facial droop
R side full strength
L hemiplegic
Incision c/d/i with sutures

Pertinent Results:
09-03 CXR: FINDINGS: Right PICC terminates within the right upper
extremity, within the upper third of the arm, as communicated by
telephone to nurse 76516 James Key Suite 473
Port Stephanie, DC 11613-0386 at 9:00 p.m. on 2015-03-20.
Tracheostomy tube remains in standard position.
Cardiomediastinal contours are within normal limits for
technique, and lungs appear grossly clear.

06-04 CT Head: IMPRESSION: Interval right cranioplasty without
evidence for acute intracranial hemorrhage or shift of normally
midline structures. Mild ex vacuo dilation of the right lateral
ventricle is again noted, consistent with right-sided volume
loss.

07-17 LENI's: no DVT


Brief Hospital Course:
Patient was admitted to Neurosurgery on 09-03 for preoperative
workup.  on 2005-06-02 and underwent the above stated procedure.
Please review dictated operative report for details.  Patient
was extubated without incident and transferred to ICU in stable
condition.  A repeat head Ct was obtained on 06-04 which showed
only post-operative changes and interval right craniopasty.
Hemovac was discontinued and removed in routine fashion.  She
was then transferred to floor in stable condition.
On 02-08 she was started on a heparin gtt and PO coumadin per CT
Paige recs. She worked with PT but was noted to be orthostatic so
she was given an IVF bolus.
On 07-17 her heparin gtt was increased. She complained of left leg
pain (calf to hip) so LENI's were ordered. Urine culture from
09-03 & 10-05 resulted + ecoli so she was started on cefepime for
10 days duration. A speech and swallow consult was requested
since patient's trach was previously capped and she was taking
PO.

On 03-27, patient PTT was 65.7, coumadin 7.5mg was given and her
trach was changed to a #6 portex cuffed. Ortho was consulted for
bilateral knee pain, x-rays were ordered and showed no fracture
or dislocation. Pain still persisted, pelvic films were ordered.
On 01-04, patient remained stable. PTT was elevated at 104 and
heparin gtt was held x 1 hour and restarted at 1450 units per
hour. She was discharged to rehab with heparin gtt and coumadin
with goal PTT 60-80 and INR of 2.5-3.5.


Medications on Admission:
Valproate Sodium, Keppra

Discharge Medications:
1. docusate sodium 50 mg/5 mL Liquid [**Month/Day (4) **]: One (1)  PO BID (2
times a day).
2. levothyroxine 50 mcg Tablet [**Month/Day (4) **]: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).
3. miconazole nitrate 2 % Powder [**Month/Day (4) **]: One (1) Appl Topical TID
(3 times a day).
4. acetaminophen 650 mg/20.3 mL Solution [**Month/Day (4) **]: One (1)  PO Q4H
(every 4 hours) as needed for Pain.
5. albuterol sulfate 90 mcg/actuation HFA Aerosol Inhaler [**Month/Day (4) **]:
2-4 Puffs Inhalation Q4H (every 4 hours).
6. amiodarone 200 mg Tablet [**Month/Day (4) **]: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).
7. simvastatin 10 mg Tablet [**Month/Day (4) **]: Two (2) Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).
8. levetiracetam 100 mg/mL Solution [**Month/Day (4) **]: Five (5)  PO BID (2
times a day).
9. aspirin 81 mg Tablet, Chewable [**Month/Day (4) **]: One (1) Tablet, Chewable
PO DAILY (Daily).
10. tramadol 50 mg Tablet [**Month/Day (4) **]: One (1) Tablet PO Q6H (every 6
hours) as needed for pain.
11. furosemide 40 mg Tablet [**Month/Day (4) **]: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).
12. lansoprazole 30 mg Tablet,Rapid Dissolve, DR Beasley: One (1)
Tablet,Rapid Dissolve, DR Beasley DAILY (Daily).
13. bisacodyl 5 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Beasley: Two (2)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO DAILY (Daily).
14. senna 8.8 mg/5 mL Syrup Beasley: Five (5) ML PO BID (2 times a
day).
15. metoprolol tartrate 25 mg Tablet Beasley: 0.5 Tablet PO TID (3
times a day).
16. insulin regular human 100 unit/mL Solution Beasley: One (1)
Injection ASDIR (AS DIRECTED).
17. glucagon (human recombinant) 1 mg Recon Soln Beasley: One (1)
Recon Soln Injection Q15MIN () as needed for hypoglycemia
protocol.
18. CefePIME 1 g IV Q12H Duration: 10 Days
19. Ondansetron 4 mg IV Q8H:PRN nausea
20. Sodium Chloride 0.9%  Flush 10 mL IV PRN line flush
Mid-line, non-heparin dependent:    Flush with 10 mL Normal
Saline daily and PRN per lumen.
21. Dextrose 50% 12.5 gm IV PRN hypoglycemia protocol
22. heparin (porcine) in D5W 25,000 unit/250 mL Parenteral
Solution Beasley: 1450 (1450) units Intravenous qhour: Please check
PTT Q6H, tritrate PTT to goal 60-80. Stop heparin when INR
theraputic 2.5-3.5.


Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
Via Christi Hospital St. Joseph Southside Regional Medical Center Hospital - 8781 Nicholas Glens
Cruzfurt, MT 71217-5033

Discharge Diagnosis:
cranial defect
Right MCA infarct


Discharge Condition:
Mental Status: Clear and coherent.
Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive.
Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.


Discharge Instructions:
??????	Exercise should be limited to walking; no lifting, straining,
or excessive bending.
??????	You may wash your hair only after sutures and/or staples have
been removed. If your wound closure uses dissolvable sutures,
you must keep that area dry for 10 days.
??????	You may shower before this time using a shower cap to cover
your head.
??????	Increase your intake of fluids and fiber, as narcotic pain
medicine can cause constipation. We generally recommend taking
an over the counter stool softener, such as Docusate (Colace)
while taking narcotic pain medication.
??????	You have been started coumadin with an INR goal 09-15
??????	If you have been discharged on Keppra (Levetiracetam), you
will not require blood work monitoring.

CALL YOUR SURGEON IMMEDIATELY IF YOU EXPERIENCE ANY OF THE
FOLLOWING

??????	New onset of tremors or seizures.
??????	Any confusion or change in mental status.
??????	Any numbness, tingling, weakness in your extremities.
??????	Pain or headache that is continually increasing, or not
relieved by pain medication.
??????	Any signs of infection at the wound site: redness, swelling,
tenderness, or drainage.
??????	Fever greater than or equal to 101?????? F.


Followup Instructions:
Follow-Up Appointment Instructions

??????Please return to the office in 14 days(from your date of
surgery) for removal of your staples/sutures and/or a wound
check. This appointment can be made with the Nurse Practitioner.
 Please make this appointment by calling 435-247-8970. If you
live quite a distance from our office, please make arrangements
for the same, with your PCP.
??????Please call (276-393-5365 to schedule an appointment with Dr.
Zachary, to be seen in 6 weeks.
??????You will need a CT scan of the brain without contrast.
